task_completion.description: Verify that the USLCI database and TRACI 2.1 methods are imported into openLCA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the openLCA application window, look at the 'Navigation' panel on the left. Verify that the USLCI database and TRACI 2.1 methods are visible and active.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the target process named 'US Electricity Grid Mix 2035'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a process tab or an entry in the navigation tree named exactly 'US Electricity Grid Mix 2035' within openLCA.

task_completion.description: Verify the process has the correct output amount and the specified input mix ratios.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Inputs/Outputs' tab of the 'US Electricity Grid Mix 2035' process, verify the quantitative reference (output) is 1 kWh (or 3.6 MJ). Verify the inputs section contains four entries: Wind Onshore (0.4 kWh), Solar PV (0.3 kWh), Nuclear (0.2 kWh), and Natural Gas (0.1 kWh), or their exact MJ equivalents.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Global Warming Potential (GWP) was calculated using the TRACI 2.1 method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an openLCA 'Results' tab for the product system. Verify that the LCIA method used is TRACI 2.1 and that the 'Global Warming Air' or 'Global Warming Potential' impact category is displayed with a calculated result.

task_completion.description: Verify the LCIA results were exported to the specified CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager, terminal window, or openLCA export dialog showing the file 'grid_2035_results.csv' located in the '/home/ga/LCA_Results/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the process composition documentation was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal window showing the file 'mix_composition.txt' in '/home/ga/LCA_Results/', containing the exact names of the four USLCI processes used.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the openLCA GUI to model the scenario.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that screenshots show the openLCA graphical interface being actively used to build the process and run the LCIA, rather than just terminal commands generating mock files.

integrity.description: Ensure the inputs are actual processes from the USLCI database.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the openLCA Inputs/Outputs tab, verify that the input processes have provider links, database icons, or location tags indicating they are genuine processes selected from the USLCI database, not just manually typed text strings.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the calculation by hardcoding fake results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal or editor windows to ensure the agent didn't use a script (like Python or bash echo) to create 'grid_2035_results.csv' with fabricated numbers without actually running the TRACI 2.1 calculation in openLCA.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target process name: 'US Electricity Grid Mix 2035'. Output: 1 kWh (or 3.6 MJ). Inputs must be from the USLCI database with the following ratios: 40% Wind Onshore (0.4 kWh), 30% Solar PV (0.3 kWh), 20% Nuclear (0.2 kWh), 10% Natural Gas (0.1 kWh). The LCIA method must be TRACI 2.1, specifically targeting the Global Warming Potential (GWP) impact category. Expected output files are '/home/ga/LCA_Results/grid_2035_results.csv' and '/home/ga/LCA_Results/mix_composition.txt'.
